restaurants.name: Taverna Saita
restaurants.ambience: Traditional Greek taverna with checkered tablecloths, stone walls, and warm, cozy lighting
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Restaurant Scholarhio
restaurants.ambience: Lively, no-frills tavern with colorful plates, casual seating, and a fun meze (small plates) concept
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Ydria
restaurants.ambience: A spacious, refreshing garden (smoking and non-smoking zones) right in the tourist heart
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Traditional food Diodos
restaurants.ambience: Outdoor terrace with direct views of the Temple of Hephaestus and a shady, tree-lined walkway
highlights: Direct views of Temple of Hephaestus
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Sin Athina
restaurants.ambience: Rooftop terrace with panoramic Acropolis and Agora views, boho-chic decor
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Attalos Restaurant & Café
restaurants.ambience: Rooftop terrace with unobstructed views of the Acropolis, Ancient Agora, and Temple of Hephaestus – casual but beautiful
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Savvas Restaurant
restaurants.ambience: Rooftop with breathtaking views of the Acropolis, casual but lively
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Oineas Restaurant
restaurants.ambience: Vintage, eclectic decor with cozy indoor and charming outdoor tables under lights
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Efcharis
restaurants.ambience: Classic taverna ambiance with outdoor seating in a pedestrian zone, often with live music
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Bulacan Halo-Halo
restaurants.ambience: All you can eat Filipino restaurant
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Tsiftis Gastro Koutouki
restaurants.ambience: Stylish yet cozy gastro vibe, indoor-outdoor seating
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Taverna Restaurant Papagiannis
restaurants.ambience: Classic taverna ambiance with outdoor seating in a pedestrian zone, often with live music
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Karavitis Tavern
restaurants.ambience: Rustic, open-air courtyard with a welcoming, family-style vibe; lots of sharing plates and a casual atmosphere
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Vyrinis Greek Tavern
restaurants.ambience: Cozy, family-run courtyard tavern with modern touches—barrels, strings of lights, and a warm, buzzing local feel
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Magemenos Avlos (The Magic Flute)
restaurants.ambience: Nostalgic taverna with live rebetika performances, oak tables & a 1960s-era vibe
must_try_dishes: A la carte meze & grilled meats
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: To Laini Kafeneio
restaurants.ambience: Traditional Cretan "kafeneio" (coffeehouse) style with a relaxed, village‑tavern feel; indoor and small outdoor seating
must_try_dishes: Dakos salad with tomato, feta, olives
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: Taverna at Bios
restaurants.ambience: Trendy Bauhaus‑style space with a vaulted interior and outdoor seating; often hosts cultural events
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

restaurants.name: To Piroliki Restaurant
restaurants.ambience: Intimate, home-style taverna with friendly service, rustic tables, and casual seating
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Lycabettus Hill
attractions.description: Panoramic views of the city and sea with the best photo opportunities at golden hour
attractions.entry_fee: €12 for a round trip or €9 for a one-way trip via funicular
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Plaka neighborhood
attractions.description: Walk through Plaka and explore neoclassical houses, quaint shops, and tavernas
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Ancient Agora & Temple of Hephaestus
attractions.description: Center of ancient public life- Ancient Agora and Temple of Hephaestus
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: National Archaeological Museum
attractions.description: Home to iconic Greek artifacts like the Mask of Agamemnon
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM- 08:00 PM with the exception of Tuesdays, when it opens at 1:00 PM
attractions.entry_fee: €12 during the peak season (April to October) and €6 during the off-season (November to March)
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Monastiraki Square
attractions.description: Wander through the flea market, dinner with a view of the Acropolis lit up at night
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Acropolis
attractions.description: Explore the Parthenon, Erechtheion, and Temple of Athena Nike
attractions.hours: During the summer (April 1 to September 15), 8:00 AM to 7:30 PM. In the winter (November 1 to March 31), 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Acropolis Museum
attractions.description: One of the best museums in Greece, enjoy the glass floors and Acropolis view from inside
attractions.hours: Generally opens at 9:00 AM daily. During summer (April 1st to October 31st), closes at 5:00 PM on Mondays, and 8:00 PM on Tuesdays through Sundays, with extended hours to 10:00 PM on Fridays. In winter (November 1st to March 31st), closes at 5:00 PM on Mondays through Thursdays, 10:00 PM on Fridays, and 8:00 PM on Saturdays and Sundays
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Panathenaic Stadium
attractions.description: Original Olympic site, made entirely of marble
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

attractions.name: Keramikos District
attractions.description: Trendy, artsy neighborhood with cafes and murals, visit the ancient cemetery or grab dinner at a modern bistro
source_files: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

hotels.name: Airotel stratos Vassilikos
hotels._source_file: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf
